rules.title: Simple substance
rules.description: In a simple substance, the oxidation state of the atom is 0.

rules.title: Fluorine: always −1
rules.description: Fluorine has the highest electronegativity, so it is always −1 in all compounds.

rules.title: Alkali metals: +1
rules.description: Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr are always +1 in compounds.

rules.title: Alkaline earth metals: +2
rules.description: Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra are always +2 in compounds.

rules.title: Oxygen: usually −2
rules.description: In most compounds, oxygen is −2. Exceptions: peroxides (−1) and compounds with F.

rules.title: Oxygen in peroxides: −1
rules.description: In peroxides, oxygen is −1 (exception to the −2 rule).

rules.title: Hydrogen: usually +1
rules.description: In most compounds, hydrogen is +1. Exception: metal hydrides (−1).

rules.title: Hydrogen in metal hydrides: −1
rules.description: In alkali and alkaline earth metal hydrides, hydrogen is −1.

rules.title: Algebraic calculation
rules.description: The unknown oxidation state is found from the condition that the sum of all oxidation states equals 0. For example: KMnO4 → Mn = +7, H2SO4 → S = +6.

rules.title: Sum of oxidation states = 0
rules.description: In a neutral molecule, the sum of oxidation states of all atoms equals 0.
